function.name: server_info
function.description: Report the Tuxlink app name/version and the live egress-authority state (armed, tainted). Read-only; does not transmit or change any state.

function.name: backend_status
function.description: Report the Winlink backend (CMS engine) connection status: connected, transport, and state. Read-only.

function.name: modem_get_status
function.description: Report the current modem (ARDOP/VARA/packet) status: kind, connected, and state. Read-only.

function.name: vara_status
function.description: Report VARA modem status: connected, bandwidth, and state. Read-only.

function.name: position_status
function.description: Report THE OPERATOR'S current station location â their Maidenhead grid square (precision-reduced to ~4 chars for privacy) and GPS fix status. Call this to answer 'where am I', 'what's near me', or any location-relative question â do NOT ask the operator for their location; Tuxlink already knows it. Read-only.

function.name: platform_info
function.description: Report host platform info (OS, arch, app version) for diagnostics. Read-only.

function.name: rig_status
function.description: Report the rig's configured state and, best-effort, its live VFO frequency/mode/PTT via a transient rigctld read (never transmits; may report nulls if the rig is unconfigured or its serial is busy with an active session). Read-only.

function.name: get_wizard_completed
function.description: Report whether the first-run setup wizard has been completed. Read-only.

function.name: p2p_peer_password_status
function.description: Report whether a P2P peer password is Set (true) or NotSet (false) for the given callsign. Never returns the password itself. Read-only.

function.name: mailbox_list
function.description: List message metadata in a mailbox folder. Returns untrusted message senders/subjects, so calling this taints the session (send authority is locked until the operator re-arms).

function.name: message_read
function.description: Read a parsed message (headers, body, attachment metadata) by folder + id. Returns untrusted content, so calling this taints the session.

function.name: user_folders_list
function.description: Enumerate mailbox folders and their message counts. Structural metadata; does not taint. Read-only.

function.name: tauri_search_run
function.description: Search mailbox messages by query, optionally scoped to a folder and limited. Returns untrusted message content, so calling this taints the session.

function.name: docs_search
function.description: Search the in-app documentation by query. App-owned content; does not taint. Read-only.

function.name: catalog_list
function.description: List the Winlink Request Center query catalog: the on-demand products the operator can request over Winlink. Hundreds of items across categories such as PROPAGATION (HF propagation forecasts), METAR (airport weather), SAT_KEPS (satellite keplerian elements), AURORA, marine/ice forecasts, and ARES/RACES bulletins. Each entry has a category, a filename id (pass these ids to catalog_send_inquiry to stage a request), and a description. This is what to browse to answer 'what can I pull/request from the Request Center'; it is NOT limited to weather/GRIB. App-owned content; does not taint. Read-only.

function.name: config_read
function.description: Read the curated, non-secret top-level config (CMS connect flag, transport, host, callsign, 4-char grid). Read-only; no passwords.

function.name: config_get_ardop
function.description: Read the non-secret ARDOP modem config (host, port, drive level, bandwidth). Read-only.

function.name: config_get_vara
function.description: Read the non-secret VARA modem config (host, port, bandwidth, drive level). Read-only; no VARA license secrets.

function.name: packet_config_get
function.description: Read the non-secret packet (AX.25/KISS) config (KISS host/port, baud, TX delay). Read-only.

function.name: config_get_rig
function.description: Read the non-secret radio-level rig config (hamlib model, rigctld endpoint, CAT serial, close-serial/live-vfo/qsy flags). Read-only; no secrets.

function.name: packet_list_serial_devices
function.description: Enumerate serial devices available for a TNC/CAT connection. Read-only.

function.name: packet_list_bluetooth_devices
function.description: Enumerate Bluetooth devices (names + minimized MACs). Read-only.

function.name: ardop_list_audio_devices
function.description: Enumerate capture + playback audio devices for modem audio selection. Read-only.

function.name: session_log_snapshot
function.description: Snapshot the current session log. May contain untrusted wire content, so calling this taints the session.

function.name: find_stations
function.description: List Winlink RMS gateways for the given transports/bands (callsign, frequencies, grid, last-heard). Public directory data, cached. Read-only; does not transmit.

modes.description: Restrict to these transports; empty = all.

bands.description: Restrict to these amateur bands e.g. "40m"; empty = all.

history_hours.description: Only gateways heard within this many hours.

function.name: predict_path
function.description: Predict HF path reliability/SNR/MUFday by UTC hour from your station to a target grid across the given dials. Offline VOACAP. Read-only.

gateway_antenna.description: Target gateway antenna type, if known.

function.name: solar_conditions
function.description: Report current space-weather indices (SFI/A/K) + the sunspot number used for predictions. Public data. Read-only.

function.name: cms_connect
function.description: Connect to the configured CMS (Winlink common message server). EGRESS: requires armed send-authority and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: verify_cms_connection
function.description: Verify the live CMS connection with a network round-trip. EGRESS: requires armed send-authority and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: rig_tune
function.description: Tune the rig to a frequency (set VFO + data mode) over CAT. EGRESS (commands the radio â same authority class as a transmit): requires armed send-authority and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: ardop_connect
function.description: Connect the ARDOP modem to a target station. Optional freq_hz pre-tunes the rig over CAT for the dial; optional qsy_candidates supplies an ordered frequency walk (operator-gated). EGRESS (keys the transmitter): requires armed send-authority and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: ardop_b2f_exchange
function.description: Run an ARDOP B2F message exchange with a target for the given routing intent (cms/radio-only/post-office/mesh/p2p; defaults to cms). The ARDOP link is tuned at connect, so this carries no frequency/QSY params. EGRESS (keys the transmitter): requires armed send-authority and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: vara_b2f_exchange
function.description: Run a VARA B2F message exchange with a target for the given routing intent (cms/radio-only/post-office/mesh/p2p; defaults to cms). Optional freq_hz / qsy_candidates pre-tune + QSY-walk the dial (VARA connects + tunes + exchanges in one call). EGRESS (keys the transmitter): requires armed send-authority and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: packet_connect
function.description: Connect an AX.25 packet session to a callsign over an optional digipeater path. EGRESS (keys the transmitter): requires armed send-authority and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: cms_abort
function.description: Abort/disconnect the CMS connection. Stopping is always allowed (never gated).

function.name: modem_ardop_disconnect
function.description: Disconnect the ARDOP modem. Stopping is always allowed (never gated).

function.name: vara_stop_session
function.description: Stop the active VARA session. Stopping is always allowed (never gated).

function.name: config_set_ardop
function.description: Set the ARDOP drive level (0..=100). WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise. An out-of-range value is rejected as invalid even when disarmed.

function.name: config_set_vara
function.description: Set the VARA bandwidth in Hz (500/2300/2750). WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise. An out-of-range value is rejected as invalid even when disarmed.

function.name: packet_config_set
function.description: Set the packet (AX.25/KISS) connection params (ssid, tcp host/port, tx delay). WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: config_set_grid
function.description: Set the station grid square (Maidenhead locator). WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: position_set_source
function.description: Set the position source (e.g. gps/manual). WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: config_set_privacy
function.description: Set the GPS privacy: broadcast state + position precision. WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: packet_set_listen
function.description: Enable or disable packet listen mode. WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: mailbox_move
function.description: Move a message between mailbox folders. WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: message_attachment_save
function.description: Save a message attachment to a destination path (validated to stay inside the attachment base; absolute/parent-traversal/escaping paths are rejected as invalid). WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

function.name: message_send
function.description: Stage a composed message in the local outbox; returns the staged message id. No transmission occurs until a later gated connect. Validates recipients/subject/body; a malformed value (e.g. a CR/LF in an address) is rejected as invalid.

function.name: send_form
function.description: Stage a standard ICS/Winlink form (e.g. ICS-213) into the local outbox. form = form type (e.g. "ICS-213"); to = recipient/role (e.g. "Logistics"); body = the message/request text (include quantities, resource names, and details). Staging is local and always available regardless of send authority.

function.name: catalog_send_inquiry
function.description: Stage a Winlink Request Center inquiry for one or more catalog item ids (the filename ids from catalog_list) in the local outbox; returns the staged message id. Use this to request ANY catalog product â propagation forecasts, METAR airport weather, satellite keplerian data, bulletins, marine forecasts, etc., not just GRIB weather. The requested products are delivered to the mailbox after the operator next connects (Arm to send). No transmission occurs here.

function.name: grib_send_request
function.description: Stage a GRIB weather-product request in the local outbox; returns the staged message id. No transmission occurs until a later gated connect. Validates the subject header.

function.name: aprs_list_stations
function.description: List stations currently on the APRS tactical map: callsign, Maidenhead grid, lat/lon, minutes since last heard, course, speed, and beacon comment. Structured position telemetry for 'where is X', 'who's on the map', or synthesizing a situational report. Read-only; does not transmit.

callsigns.description: Restrict to these callsigns; empty = all heard stations.

since_min.description: Only stations heard within this many minutes.

function.name: aprs_station_track
function.description: Return the recent position history (track) for one APRS station: an ordered series of timestamped grid/lat/lon/course/speed fixes for synthesizing movement. Read-only; does not transmit.

callsign.description: The station whose track to return.

function.name: aprs_read_messages
function.description: Read inbound APRS text messages addressed to the operator (tactical chat / field-team traffic). Returns sender, timestamp, and free-text body. UNTRUSTED CONTENT: reading it TAINTS the session (an APRS message can carry injected instructions), so egress and Tier-2 writes are denied afterward until a clean session. Read-only; does not transmit.

callsign.description: Restrict to messages from this station; empty = all senders.

since_min.description: Only messages received within this many minutes.

function.name: aprs_send_message
function.description: Send a message over APRS. With `to` set, sends an addressed APRS message to that station; with `to` omitted, broadcasts to the open tactical-chat channel (all stations). Payload is capped at 67 characters (APRS limit) — an over-length text is rejected as invalid even when disarmed. EGRESS (keys the transmitter): the actual send requires armed send-authority and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

to.description: Destination callsign for an addressed APRS message; omit to broadcast to tactical chat.

function.name: config_set_transport
function.description: Set the CMS transport and endpoint: kind (telnet / cms-ssl / packet / vara / ardop), host (e.g. an AREDN mesh hostname for a Telnet Post Office), and routing_intent (cms / post-office / radio-only / p2p / mesh). WRITE: requires armed send-authority (Tier-2 remediation) and an un-tainted session; denied otherwise.

host.description: Endpoint host (hostname or IP); e.g. an AREDN mesh address.
